headline: Inflation seen peaking in May, falling in June
detail: Kalshi prediction markets expect energy-driven inflation relief in June. Cooling inflation reduces pressure on the Fed to stay hawkish, which is historically a direct tailwind for high-multiple tech and semiconductor names - exactly your book. This is the most positive macro signal in today's headlines for your positions.
rec: No immediate trade needed, but if SMH or SOXL show strength on this narrative, it could be a reason to add to SMH (not SOXL - decay risk too high). Watch SMH above its current level before committing given margin exposure.

headline: Oil falls on U.S.-Iran talks progress
detail: Lower oil directly feeds into the inflation-peaking narrative, reinforcing the soft macro read above. For your tech-heavy book, cheaper energy means lower input costs and less Fed pressure - both positive. No direct chip/software catalyst, but it supports the broad tape staying constructive.
rec: This backstops the positive macro read but doesn't give you a specific entry point in any holding. No trade warranted on its own - let it support your existing HOLDs.

headline: USMCA non-renewal opens trade renegotiation
detail: Dropping USMCA adds uncertainty to auto and manufacturing supply chains, but your book is almost entirely software and semiconductors. Indirect risk exists if broader market sentiment sours on trade-war fears, which could pressure QQQ and SPY. Chip equipment names could see noise if trade friction escalates toward Asia-linked supply chains.
rec: No action today, but keep an eye on QQQ ($670 stop) and SMH ($540 stop) if trade headlines escalate. Your stops are well below current prices so you have room - just don't ignore it if rhetoric heats up.

headline: Retail traders smashing Wall Street records
detail: Heavy retail participation often signals late-stage momentum in crowded names - many of which overlap your holdings (CRWD, chip ETFs). This is a sentiment warning, not a sell signal today, but crowded retail longs can unwind fast when sentiment shifts, and leverage amplifies that for you specifically.
rec: No trade, but this is a reason to keep your stops tight and resist the urge to add size. Your margin exposure means a crowded-trade unwind hits you harder than most.
